.@SecPompeo on the U.S.-Australia alliance: Our two great democracies face immediate crises like the COVID-19 pandemic and longer-term challenges like the Chinese Communist Party’s ambitions. We are lucky to count Australia as a close partner through it all. #USwithAUS https://t.co/QzvGylhYeh

.@SecPompeo: The only way to truly change communist China is to act [on the basis of] how they behave. President Reagan said that he dealt with the Soviet Union on the basis of “trust but verify.” When it comes to the CCP, I say we must distrust and verify. https://t.co/HnulEdhN4H

Since 2000, 154 countries have criminalized all forms of human trafficking. As we fight to #EndTrafficking we continue to work with countries around the world to make a positive difference. Learn what governments are doing to fight trafficking: https://t.co/gkjK7jwLd0. https://t.co/B3HHE0Nw6F

.@JTIP_State: Ambassador Richmond: 20 years ago, we did not have a UN protocol against trafficking in persons. Now 178 countries have joined as parties to the protocol. This protocol now stands as one of the most widely adopted international legal instruments. #EndTrafficking https://t.co/zs8fT9HiSx

The U.S. is committed to ending all forms of modern slavery, as seen in our commitment to the Trafficking in Persons report. We remain steadfast in declaring that there is no excuse and governments must take action to #EndHumanTrafficking in all its forms. https://t.co/vIXFER6gL9

On World Day against Trafficking in Persons & every day, the Department works to promote and protect the rights of trafficking victims.
